title: gnaphalium unionis var. unionis
intro: Gnaphalium unionis var. unionis, also known as the alpine cudweed, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and reduce swelling. The plant is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, offering potential health benefits such as antioxidant, antiviral, and antimicrobial activities, which can help protect against infections and diseases. Traditionally, Gnaphalium unionis var. unionis has been used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.
